message:
refactor(mcp): normalize framework-neutral tool and content types at client boundary

Introduce McpClientToolDefinition and McpClientContentItem wrapper types
to decouple the MCP client interface from framework-specific types at
the serialization boundary.

McpClientToolDefinition provides:
- Framework-neutral storage of name, description, input/output schemas,
  icon, version, tags, and annotations
- from_framework() / into_framework() conversion with error propagation
- First-class is_destructive() flag detection from annotations

McpClientContentItem provides:
- Newtype wrapping serde_json::Value for type-safe content passing
- as_text() helper for extracting text content
- Bidirectional framework conversion

Update list_tools() and call_tool() return types to use the new wrapper
types, removing the leaked framework dependency from the public API.
Remove into_client() method that exposed the raw framework client.

In mcp_proxy.rs, harden tool composition and execution paths:
- RemoteProxyToolHandler::new() now returns McpClientResult<Self> and
  validates tool conversion upfront
- compose_proxy_tools() catches mapping failures and either fails fast
  or logs warnings and skips the problematic tool
- filter_remote_tools() uses is_destructive() instead of manual
  annotation traversal
- Tool execution converts McpClientContentItem back to framework types
  with detailed error logging on conversion failure

Add roundtrip_framework_payload() and framework_payload_error() utility
functions for standardized JSON serialization error handling.

Includes test: tool_definition_reads_destructive_annotation()

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
